candidate_problem_record.subject: math
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, $AB \parallel DE$, and points $B$, $C$, $D$ are on the same line. If $\angle BCE = 55^\circ$ and $\angle E = 25^\circ$, then what is the measure of $\angle B$?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $\angle BCE=55^\circ$ and $\angle E=25^\circ$, we have $\angle BCE=\angle E+\angle D$. \\
Therefore, $\angle D=\angle BCE-\angle E=55^\circ-25^\circ=30^\circ$. \\
Thus, $AB\parallel DE$. \\
Consequently, $\angle B=\angle D$. \\
Hence, $\angle B=\boxed{30^\circ}$.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集被视为具备较稳定的多解潜力，可进入更强的多解挖掘链路。
